Title: EIL (ethylene-insensitive 3-like) transcription factors in apple affect both ethylene- and cold response-dependent fruit ripening

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in ripening fruit at harvest and after cold treatment; very low in MdEIL co-suppressed lines and ACO1as
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest maturity and after 10 weeks cold treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment (10 weeks at 3 or 5°C); ethylene signaling status
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced MdACO1 expression is associated with strongly reduced ethylene production in fruit.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACO1 transcript abundance matched fruit ethylene production patterns. 2. Expression was very low in MdEIL co-suppressed lines and in ACO1as fruit at both harvest and after cold treatment. 3. MdACO1 was weakly upregulated by cold treatment across lines.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transgenic antisense comparison; ethylene measurement
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower ethylene is associated with greener skin, delayed softening, and reduced aroma volatile production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper MdACO1 functions mainly as a downstream marker/effector of MdEIL-mediated ripening rather than the primary experimental focus gene.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to modulate ripening rate and storage life, but likely with penalties in aroma and color development.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major apple aroma volatile contributing to ripe fruit aroma
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in ripening fruit; reduced in MdEIL co-suppressed lines at harvest and after cold treatment; restored to control level in ACO1as after cold treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest maturity and after 10 weeks cold treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment; ethylene treatment in transient assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdAFS1 expression promotes α-farnesene production during apple ripening and cold-responsive ripening.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdAFS1 expression was significantly reduced in MdEIL co-suppressed fruit at harvest and after cold treatment. 2. Its promoter was directly activated by MdEIL1, MdEIL3 and MdEIL4, with MdEIL3 strongest. 3. EMSA showed MdEIL1–3 bind a 32 bp MdAFS1 promoter fragment containing an EIL-binding site. 4. Low MdAFS1 expression correlated with low α-farnesene emission.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; GC-MS volatile profiling; dual-luciferase promoter assay; promoter deletion assay; EMSA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower expression reduces characteristic ripe-fruit aroma.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq read mapping mentioned for 5'UTR identification; accession NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper demonstrates direct promoter targeting by MdEIL TFs, making MdAFS1 a key downstream effector for aroma formation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving apple aroma, especially together with regulators such as MdEILs; caution that aroma enhancement may couple with faster ripening.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall loosening contributes to fruit softening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: lower in MdEIL co-suppressed lines at harvest and after cold treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest maturity and after cold treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment; ethylene treatment in transient assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced MdXTH1 expression is associated with reduced fruit softening.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdXTH1 expression was significantly reduced in MdEIL co-suppressed fruit at both stages. 2. The promoter was activated by MdEIL3 and MdEIL4, and more weakly by MdEIL1. 3. Its repression parallels firmer fruit in MdEIL co-suppressed lines.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; firmness measurement; dual-luciferase promoter assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased softening may shorten storage life; reduced expression may impair eating texture development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is one of the principal validated downstream targets of MdEILs in Results.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune firmness retention during storage, ideally balancing texture and flavor.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pectin/galactan remodeling contributes to fruit softening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: lower in MdEIL co-suppressed lines at harvest and after cold treatment; control-level in ACO1as
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest maturity and after cold treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment; ethylene treatment in transient assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced MdbGAL expression is associated with reduced softening in MdEIL co-suppressed fruit.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdbGAL transcripts were reduced in MdEIL co-suppressed lines at harvest and after cold treatment. 2. MdEIL3 and MdEIL4 significantly activated the promoter, with weaker activation by MdEIL1. 3. Promoter deletion suggested EIL-responsive region exists but exact direct binding site remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; firmness measurement; dual-luciferase promoter assay; promoter deletion assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher activity may accelerate softening and reduce storability.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq read mapping mentioned for 5'UTR identification; accession NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding was not proven for MdbGAL, unlike MdAFS1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for firmness engineering, especially in combination with ethylene regulators.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: lower in MdEIL co-suppressed lines and ACO1as at harvest; in ACO1as returns to control level after cold treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest maturity and after cold treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment; ethylene treatment in transient assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower MdoOMT1 expression is associated with reduced estragole production at harvest; cold can restore promoter responsiveness in transient assay.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdoOMT1 expression was reduced in MdEIL co-suppressed fruit at harvest and after cold treatment. 2. In ACO1as, expression was reduced at harvest but not after cold treatment. 3. The promoter was not activated by MdEILs at room temperature, but MdEIL3 activated it under cold treatment.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; GC-MS volatile profiling; dual-luciferase promoter assay under ambient and cold conditions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter desirable aroma balance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulation appears condition-dependent and may be indirect or cold-facilitated.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tailoring aroma in cold-stored apple fruit, but should be combined with broader aroma-network regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ethylene-dependent aroma volatiles and ripening-associated products including α-farnesene; also affects ethylene output
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls ripening traits including aroma formation, softening, and cold-responsive ripening
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fruit ripening-associated ethylene biosynthesis, aroma biosynthesis, and cell wall remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator of ethylene- and cold response-dependent fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in fruit at harvest; one of four fruit-expressed MdEIL genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest maturity and after cold treatment; overexpressed in E317ox and E322ox, co-suppressed in E310ko and E316ko
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment; exogenous ethylene in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances ethylene production and softening, while co-suppression reduces ethylene, aroma volatile production, and softening and blocks cold-responsive ripening outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Stable 35S:MdEIL1 transformation generated overexpression and co-suppressed lines. 2. Co-suppressed lines had reduced MdEIL1-4 expression, low ethylene, firmer fruit and lower volatile aroma production. 3. Overexpression line E317ox showed enhanced ripening and more ethylene. 4. MdEIL1 directly activated the MdAFS1, MdXTH1 and weakly MdbGAL promoters. 5. Co-suppression prevented cold induction of several cold-response genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression/co-suppression lines; RT-qPCR; ethylene and firmness phenotyping; GC-MS volatile profiling; dual-luciferase promoter assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdAFS1 promoter; MdXTH1 promoter; MdbGAL promoter; MdEIL2; MdEIL3; MdEIL4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MdAFS1, MdXTH1 and MdbGAL promoters; positively associated with MdACO1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced MdEIL1 activity may accelerate ripening and softening, reducing storage life; reduced activity lowers aroma and color development.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Manipulation of MdEIL1 caused co-suppression of MdEIL2-4 because of high sequence identity, so some phenotypes reflect combined loss of fruit-expressed EILs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for tuning apple ripening and storage behavior; partial editing or allele-specific modulation may be preferable to avoid severe aroma and color penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for transgene overexpression; native target promoters in reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: contributes as part of the fruit-expressed MdEIL module, though weaker than MdEIL3/4 in promoter assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in fruit at harvest
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest and after cold treatment; co-suppressed in E310ko/E316ko
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss together with MdEIL1/3/4 is associated with reduced ripening, low aroma and low ethylene production.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdEIL2 is one of four MdEIL genes highly expressed in fruit. 2. It was co-suppressed in MdEIL1 transgenic knockdown lines. 3. EMSA showed MdEIL2 can bind the MdAFS1 EIL motif, although it did not activate tested promoters in dual-luciferase assays.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; co-suppression analysis; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds MdAFS1 promoter in vitro; no significant activation of MdAFS1, MdXTH1, or MdbGAL promoters in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of combined EIL suppression, reduced activity is linked to delayed ripening and aroma loss.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The gene was not independently manipulated; functional inference comes from co-suppression and promoter binding assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible fine-tuning target, but weaker standalone effect than MdEIL3/4 is suggested by transient assays.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-farnesene and other ripening-related aroma volatiles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes ripening-associated aroma formation and softening-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: aroma biosynthesis and cell wall modification during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: strongest tested activator among MdEIL family members for key ripening promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in fruit at harvest
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest and after cold treatment; co-suppressed in E310ko/E316ko
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold treatment; exogenous ethylene in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a major positive activator of ripening genes controlling α-farnesene formation and softening-related processes.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdEIL3 most strongly activated the MdAFS1, MdXTH1 and MdbGAL promoters. 2. MdEIL3 directly bound the MdAFS1 promoter EIL site in EMSA. 3. Cold altered MdEIL3 transactivation outcomes, including enabling activation of MdoOMT1 promoter. 4. Co-suppression of MdEIL3 with other fruit EILs correlated with strong ripening suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase promoter assay; promoter deletion assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdAFS1 promoter; MdXTH1 promoter; MdbGAL promoter; MdoOMT1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdAFS1; MdXTH1; MdbGAL; MdoOMT1 under cold
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: strongly activates MdAFS1, MdXTH1 and MdbGAL promoters; activates MdoOMT1 promoter under cold conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong activation of ripening genes may reduce firmness and storage potential.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested EILs, MdEIL3 appears to be the strongest direct transactivator of core ripening target promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulator for aroma enhancement or ripening acceleration; requires careful deployment to avoid over-softening.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native target promoters in reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports ripening-related aroma and softening regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator with substantial promoter activation capacity, second to MdEIL3 in tested assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in fruit at harvest
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit at harvest and after cold treatment; co-suppressed in E310ko/E316ko
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes positively to activation of aroma and softening-related genes as part of the fruit MdEIL module.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdEIL4 is one of four fruit-expressed MdEIL genes. 2. It significantly activated MdAFS1, MdXTH1 and MdbGAL promoters in transient assays. 3. Co-suppression together with MdEIL1-3 was associated with strong ripening defects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase promoter assay; co-suppression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdAFS1 promoter; MdXTH1 promoter; MdbGAL promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MdAFS1, MdXTH1 and MdbGAL promoters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Positive ripening regulation may compromise firmness retention if enhanced.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly overexpressed individually in stable lines; inference is from expression, co-suppression and transient activation assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complementary regulator for ripening engineering, likely useful in combination strategies rather than alone.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native target promoters in reporter assays
